reasoning: The 200m freestyle world record (1:52.23) is held by Ariarne Titmus, set in June 2024. As of October 2025, Wikipedia reflects that her record has survived the 2025 World Aquatics Championships (which concluded in August 2025) and remains intact. The period between November and January corresponds to the off-season for long-course (50m) swimming, which predominantly features short-course (25m) events such as the World Aquatics Short Course Championships. Long-course world records are very rarely broken outside of major international championships or national Olympic/World Championship trials. Given her dominant and significant drop in time from previous records, there is very little likelihood of it being broken in an obscure long-course meet during the winter season. The probability decreases slightly over time purely due to the longer hazard period, but remains strongly anchored near the upper limit allowed by the rules (0.95) for short-term swimming world record holds.
